rationale: In the citing paper, Moe and Fader (2001) appears only once in a grouped citation supporting a background claim that movies/books have short, predictable exponential life cycles. The reference is used to motivate why the authors choose a different product category, not to build the paper's core ratings-hazard framework or sales decomposition. There is no distinct discussion of the cited paper's model, findings, or constructs beyond this contextual point.
